claim: Agentic systems require feedback loops to understand agent behavior and validate success
actionable: Build spyware/logging systems to record agent conversations and add validation functions that confirm agents completed their tasks
source_title: AutoGen SPYWARE: Coding Systems for SUCCESSFUL AI Agents (Postgres Data Analytics)

claim: Product managers in multi-agent systems often add no value and waste tokens if they only validate output without concrete checks
actionable: Replace vague product manager validation with explicit validator functions that return boolean success/failure signals
source_title: AutoGen SPYWARE: Coding Systems for SUCCESSFUL AI Agents (Postgres Data Analytics)

claim: Centralized instrument/store classes for agents enable state sharing and unified function access across multi-agent systems
actionable: Create a base instrument class with shared state and methods; inherit specialized versions (e.g., PostgresAgentInstruments) for specific agent teams
source_title: AutoGen SPYWARE: Coding Systems for SUCCESSFUL AI Agents (Postgres Data Analytics)

claim: MultiAgent systems become scalable when you stack incremental improvements: validators, orchestrators, conversation tracking, and shared state
actionable: Build agentic systems iteratively by first adding logging, then validation, then state management; avoid jumping to complex systems
source_title: AutoGen SPYWARE: Coding Systems for SUCCESSFUL AI Agents (Postgres Data Analytics)

claim: Session-based tracking (using timestamp + prompt hash) enables comparison of agent system behavior across runs
actionable: Generate unique session IDs from hour:minute:second timestamps and prompt parsing; store agent outputs in session-specific directories
source_title: AutoGen SPYWARE: Coding Systems for SUCCESSFUL AI Agents (Postgres Data Analytics)
